title: Full title revealed Evangelion3010 Thrice Upon A Time
display_title: Full title revealed Evangelion3010 Thrice Upon A Time
wikitext:
[]: Full title revealed (Evangelion:3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time)             Quote Post by girlifyoudidnt » Fri Apr 17, 2020 3:30 am New movie poster! And title reveal: Evangelion 3.0+1.0: Thrice Upon a Time tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Lacissal » Fri Apr 17, 2020 3:31 am 'Thrice Upon a Time' official subtitle? Edit- just saw you'd posted that part and can't delete post Edit 2- they've switched to writing 'Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0' in the Rebuild style handwriting, when even last summer the Eiffel Tower poster had it in the old NGE font

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Joseki » Fri Apr 17, 2020 3:32 am "Bye-bye all of EVANGELION" got me hard, not gonna lie.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by girlifyoudidnt » Fri Apr 17, 2020 3:38 am Full staff credits for 3.0+1.0 revealed on the website https://www.evangelion.co.jp/final.html Message from Anno. tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Joseki » Fri Apr 17, 2020 3:42 am Thrice Upon A Time is a science fiction novel by British writer James P. Hogan, first published in 1980. Unlike most other time travel stories, Thrice Upon A Time considers the ramifications of sending messages into the past and/or receiving messages from the future, rather than the sending of physical objects through time. From Wikipedia.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Apr 17, 2020 3:51 am You better believe that almost made me cry. I correctly predicted pencil strokes for the title, yay. And about the subtitle, unexpected, yet typically Anno if it's a sci fi novel name.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Joseki » Fri Apr 17, 2020 3:54 am I think the new image is a reference to "quantum Rei" at the beginning of 1.0. The movie is going to end with Shinji seeing himself when waiting for Misato to pick him up during Sachiel's attack.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 17, 2020 4:17 am There's no train tracks anywhere in sight when the Rei appears. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Apr 17, 2020 5:28 am My mind is exploding, please wait for response.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Apr 17, 2020 5:55 am Thoughts on the staff: https://kuribo4indahouse.tumblr.com/post/615640535260151808/%E3%82%B7%E3%83%B3%E3%82%A8%E3%83%B4%E3%82%A1%E3%83%B3%E3%82%B2%E3%83%AA%E3%82%AA%E3%83%B3%E5%8A%87%E5%A0%B4%E7%89%88

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Apr 17, 2020 7:14 am “Bye-bye all of Evangelion’’. So the movie is pretty final.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Apr 17, 2020 7:50 am I love it, but it can also be interpreted as all of Evangelion being destroyed for good, which is pretty hilarious. I suspect a little bit of Engrish. After all, Anno wants there to be more Eva. It's more specifically about his Evangelion.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by amitakartok » Fri Apr 17, 2020 7:51 am I think so too. There's plenty of air left in the franchise, but this could be Anno's final work in it. He's kinda burned out.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Lacissal » Fri Apr 17, 2020 7:57 am 'Farewell, Space Battleship Misato'

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by hui43210 » Fri Apr 17, 2020 8:17 am Anyone who can translate Anno's letter? Also it's kinda mean of Khara to increase my hype for the film right after having to delay it. https://twitter.com/dehogallery/status/ ... 17793?s=19 Based on the Google translate of the tweet, looks like the staff can still work from home which is a good sign.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Apr 17, 2020 8:32 am So I read up on the Wikipedia article about Thrice Upon a Time and found this at the end: That very day, in this third iteration of the timeline, Murdoch and Lee go out shopping in Kingussie, and a certain young woman named Anne trips over Sir Charles's kitten... This is the origin of the story's title, Thrice Upon A Time.

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Apr 17, 2020 8:35 am Once again, Anno manages to subvert expectations in a way that would make even Rian Johnson blush. I don't think ANYONE on Earth could have predicted "Thrice Upon a Time" as being the official subtitle for this film. Another interpretation of the title, apart from BlueBasilisk's is that 3.0+1.0 will be the third official canon ending in the franchise. However, there could be a couple different interpretations of this. Option 1 1st Ending: NGE26 2nd Ending: EOE 3rd Ending: 3.0+1.0 Option 2 1st Ending: NGE26/EOE 2nd Ending: NGE Manga 3rd Ending: 3.0+1.0 The reason for option 2's existence is that NGE26 and EOE could be interpreted as being the same ending, given that they are both occurring within the same universe of that iteration of the franchise, albeit from different points of view. I, too am interested in the translation of Anno's letter. Has there been any indication on what the runtime of the film is going to be?

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Lacissal » Fri Apr 17, 2020 8:43 am Guy Nacks wrote: Has there been any indication on what the runtime of the film is going to be?

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Joseki » Fri Apr 17, 2020 8:48 am I think the composition with the railroad and Shinji is quite intentional

[]: Re: Shin Eva - Official Social Media             Quote Post by Velorex » Fri Apr 17, 2020 8:53 am Actually I think the path converges to Shinji.

Article: Full title revealed Evangelion3010 Thrice Upon A Time
 manages to subvert expectations in a way that would make even Rian Johnson blush. I don't think ANYONE on Earth could have predicted "Thrice Upon a Time" as being the official subtitle for this film. Another interpretation of the title, apart from BlueBasilisk's is that 3.0+1.0 will be the third official canon ending in the franchise. However, there could be a couple different interpretations of this. Option 1 1st Ending: NGE26 2nd Ending: EOE 3rd Ending: 3.0+1.0 Option 2 1st Ending: NGE26/EOE 2nd Ending: NGE Manga 3rd Ending: 3.0+1.0 The reason for option 2's existence is that NGE26 and EOE could be interpreted as being the same ending, given that they are both occurring within the same universe of that iteration of the franchise, albeit from different points of view. I, too am interested in the translation of Anno's letter. Has there been any indication on what the runtime of the film is going to be?
